steps.title: Identify the temporal relationship
steps.content: The first sentence establishes the voting phase—voters choose parties—while the second sentence describes what happens next: parties receive seats based on vote counts. The transition must signal movement from one phase of the process to the next.

steps.title: Apply to the choices
steps.content: “Then” marks this forward progression through the process. The other options introduce wrong relationships: “However” and “Conversely” suggest contrast, and “In other words” suggests restatement rather than sequence. That confirms B is correct.
